meta.source: Author analysis: task-reconciliation.csv (git+PR+attestation+deliverable cross-reference) and tool-runs/*.log — distinct from the 6 specialist-agent findings files.
meta.summary: Governance / evidence-integrity findings that emerged from the S1-18 task reconciliation and the deterministic tool-execution phase. Numbers are triple-corroborated where noted (this reconciliation + stale-artifacts.json agent + docs/CURRENT_STATE_REPORT.md).

findings.evidence: REPRODUCIBLE: `for id in PG-196 PG-197 PG-198 PG-199 PG-201..PG-209 INFRA-TF-001..005; do test -f .specify/sprints/sprint-18/attestations/$id/attestation.json; done` -> all 18 MISSING (only task-tracking.json stub present). Triple-corroborated: task-reconciliation.csv (18 Completed-without-json, all Sprint 18), stale-artifacts.json STALE-003, and docs/CURRENT_STATE_REPORT.md.
findings.impact: 18 tasks CSV-marked Completed in Sprint 18 (13 Module-Settings pages PG-196..209 + 5 Terraform INFRA-TF-001..005) shipped without a canonical attestation.json. The code exists (pages verified under apps/web/src/app), but there is no gate/KPI/provenance evidence record — ADR-068 attestation-provenance policy is unmet for these tasks. Blocks a clean Sprint-19 entry gate.
findings.rootCause: Batch UI-page and infra work marked Completed via task-tracking.json without the /exec attestation ceremony producing attestation.json.
findings.policyViolation: ADR-068 (attestation provenance standardization); DoD attestation requirement
findings.remediation: Retro-attest the 18 tasks: run the attestation ceremony (or a documented backfill with git_commit + evidence links) against the already-shipped code. Do NOT delete the code — it exists and passes typecheck/lint/build. This is an evidence backfill, not a re-implementation.

findings.evidence: REPRODUCIBLE: reconcile.mjs over 408 S1-18 tasks -> Completed=404; with attestation.json=386; with FULL provenance (run_id+timestamp+verdict+artifact_hashes|gate_results)=227; => 177 Completed tasks (44%) lack full provenance (159 have an attestation.json with incomplete provenance + 18 have none).
findings.impact: 44% of completed Sprints 1-18 work cannot be independently re-verified from its attestation alone. Most (159) are pre-ADR-068 attestations lacking git_commit/artifact_hashes; this is historical debt, not fabrication, but it means the 'delivered & proven' baseline is only 227/404 = 56%.
findings.rootCause: ADR-068 provenance standardization (run_id, artifact_hashes, git_commit) postdates most Sprint 1-16 attestations; earlier ceremony wrote thinner records.
findings.remediation: Accept pre-ADR-068 attestations as grandfathered but record the exact 177-task list in the debt ledger; enforce full provenance going forward (already the ADR-068 default). Optionally backfill git_commit for the 17 present-but-incomplete Sprint-18 attestations (cheap, high-value).

findings.evidence: REPRODUCIBLE: `pnpm validate:schemas` -> exit 1, '=== Summary === Passed: 272 Failed: 175 Total: 447'. Raw log saved at tool-runs/validate-schemas.log.
findings.impact: 175 of 447 committed JSON artifacts fail their own JSON-schema validation. The validate:schemas gate is RED on origin/main. Downstream consumers (dashboard, metrics APIs, plan-lint) may be reading structurally-invalid data; schema/data drift undermines the 'single source of truth' guarantee.
findings.rootCause: Schemas or artifact structures evolved without regenerating/validating (`pnpm run generate:schemas` referenced in the failure message). Not wired as a hard pre-ship blocker, so drift accumulated.
findings.policyViolation: ADR-012 (CSV/JSON source of truth); ADR-016 (analytics integrity)
findings.remediation: Triage the 175 failures (bucket by schema); regenerate schemas where structure legitimately changed, fix artifacts where they drifted. Then wire validate:schemas into pre-ship as a hard gate. Separate remediation PR — NOT this audit PR.

findings.evidence: REPRODUCIBLE: reconcile.mjs -> DOC-015 and DOC-016 are Status=Backlog in CSV but each has a passing attestation.json (verdict pass) and merged work (DOC-016 via PR #598). Classified status-desatualizado.
findings.impact: Two Sprint-18 tasks are done-but-marked-Backlog, understating Sprint-18 completion (true is 65/67, not 63/67) and polluting the Sprint-19 entry baseline.
findings.rootCause: CSV status flip lagged the merge (known: DOC-016 absorbed DOC-015; reconcile of docs 163->165 landed but CSV not flipped).
findings.policyViolation: ADR-012 (CSV single source of truth must be kept current)
findings.remediation: Flip DOC-015 and DOC-016 to Completed in Sprint_plan.csv and run the sync. Trivial CSV edit — belongs in a metrics-reconcile PR, not this audit PR.

findings.evidence: REPRODUCIBLE: `pnpm nplus1:scan` -> exit 1 with 1 NEW unbaselined violation (inbound.router.ts:946); `grep -rn 'nplus1' scripts/pre-ship.mjs .github/workflows` -> not wired. Same for validate:schemas (GOV-A-003).
findings.impact: Deterministic quality gates that DO catch real defects (nplus1:scan, validate:schemas) exist but are not part of pre-ship or CI required checks, so their failures (a new N+1, 175 schema breaks) shipped to main undetected.
findings.rootCause: Gates authored as standalone scripts and never promoted into the pre-ship/CI required set.
findings.policyViolation: ADR-060 (required-checks policy); ADR-053 (N+1 budget detector intent)
findings.remediation: Add nplus1:scan and validate:schemas to scripts/pre-ship.mjs (and CI required checks) once their current backlogs are green. Sequence after GOV-A-003 fix. Separate CI PR.

findings.evidence: REPRODUCIBLE: sonarcloud public API -> quality gate status OK, but every gate condition targets NEW code (new_reliability/new_security/new_maintainability/new_coverage/new_duplication/new_hotspots). Overall security_rating=D(4.0), reliability=C(3.0), 6 bugs, 1 vulnerability, 14 hotspots do NOT gate. See sonar-baseline.json.
findings.impact: Clean-as-you-code keeps NEW code clean (A ratings, 91.9% new coverage) but lets overall-code security (D) and reliability (C) debt persist invisibly behind a green gate. A reader seeing 'Quality Gate: OK' overestimates overall health.
findings.rootCause: Default SonarCloud 'Sonar way' gate is new-code-only by design.
findings.policyViolation: ADR-015 (security validation) intent vs. gate configuration
findings.remediation: Either add an overall-code security_rating>=C floor to the gate, or explicitly document (in ADR-015) that overall-security remediation is tracked via the security-findings backlog, not the gate. Track the 14 hotspots + 1 vuln to closure.

findings.evidence: REPRODUCIBLE: initial reconcile (git+PR+attestation only) flagged PG-196..208 as conclusao-fantasma; deliverable check `find apps/web/src/app -path '*<slug>*' -name page.tsx` found all 13 pages -> reclassified to entregue-sem-evidencia. Final phantom count = 0.
findings.impact: Positive/methodological: there are ZERO true phantom completions in Sprints 1-18. Every Completed task has either an attestation, a git/PR trace, or a verified deliverable. The provenance gap (GOV-A-001/002) is an evidence problem, not a fake-delivery problem.
findings.rootCause: UI page files do not embed their task ID, so commit/attestation-only reconciliation under-detects delivery; deliverable-existence check is required to avoid false 'phantom' labels.
findings.remediation: Retain the deliverable-existence check in any future reconciliation tooling to keep false-positive rate low.
